items.description: Salvaged plate and fittings — primary bench input for improvised tools, weapons, and hardware.
Bottles, pucks, and ampoules rescued from custodial and MEP lockers — propellant feedstock and flux for ballistics.
Loose pistol ammunition (no magazines). Scrap jackets plus chemical stock for primer and propellant chemistry.
Loose shells crimped from scrap cups — chemical stock feeds primers and payloads.
Cylinder lock consumed when formally claiming an apartment.
Replaceable foam insert for the apartment fish-tank filter. Scavenged from pet-supply aisles and maintenance closets — install from the filter maintenance slot for a full cartridge swap.
Yellow-banded spare fuse kit from a maintenance closet — MEP tie-in insurance for the block.
Building chits and stamped maintenance slips — the block's soft currency for favors, parts, and bench time.
